mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding real economic output, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. This is an always-on trend-following relationship with no activation threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or correlation sign flips negative in more than one calendar year out of five.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy begins to materially compress equity valuations via higher discount rates and tighter credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Below this threshold the mechanism is inactive.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, evaluated over at least three distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth jointly signal an emerging labor market downturn that neither series confirms alone; this combined deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets price in reduced consumer spending and earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth turns negative
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from the single-series UNRATE-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-sensitive expansion regimes, sustained increases in housing starts over 6 months signal robust interest-rate-sensitive demand and household confidence, which precede broader economic strength and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In contractionary or high-rate regimes this relationship reverses because housing weakness leads broader demand deterioration.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between rising HOUST and forward SPX returns fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03 or sign reversal) when FEDFUNDS is below 3%, or the effect does not attenuate as hypothesized when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month acceleration in the CPI index above trend raises inflation surprise risk, prompting near-term repricing of rate-hike expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as risk premia adjust quickly to the surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds trailing 12-month average by more than 1 standard deviation
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following a CPI surprise trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across at least 10 historical trigger events, or reaction reverses positively within the same window.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term momentum in retail sales growth reflects immediate consumer spending strength, which is rapidly incorporated into near-term equity pricing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week. This is an always-on relationship reflecting continuous consumer demand signaling with no regime dependency.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no meaningful predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with simultaneously rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy prompting policy tightening response; the interaction of strong output growth and rising rates jointly predicts a near-term negative S&P 500 forward return over the next month as markets anticipate policy-induced deceleration, a pattern not captured by either series individually.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over the FEDFUNDS-only threshold signal (H_002).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals durable economic expansion, which supports risk asset valuations and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In late-cycle or stagnation regimes, the same GDP growth rate carries diminished predictive power because markets have already priced in the expansion.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following a GDP growth trigger are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE is simultaneously declining below 5%, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable across recovery versus late-cycle subsamples.
